responses.concludingNotes:
**The game is out of the box in a acceptable state, but by spending like 20 more minutes installing ProtonGE, activating DXVK-Async and forcing dx11, the experience can be greatly improved.**  

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you have a fast GPU, and you dislike the blurry image and heavy ghosting TAA produces in this game, you should really downgrade the game to v1.001 and  [deactivate TAA](https://www.pcgamingwiki.com/wiki/Final_Fantasy_VII_Remake_Intergrade#Anti-aliasing_.28AA.29), then replace it with downsampling. The game is >50h long, so it's worth the hassle.

notes.performanceFaults:
- **Frequent hitches** (Presumably shader compilation): I swiched the game to dx11 mode and installed ProtonGE in combination with activating DXVK-Async to get much fewer hitches.
- **Bad framepacing (rare)**: I had really bad framepacing like once every 10h of gameplay. Restarting the game fixed this.

For the game to be playable anti-piracy measures have to be disabled. To do this:

responses.concludingNotes: The DLL *atigktxx.dll* from **ATi Catalyst 10.4** is needed, to start the game and remove the blackscreen!

notes.customizationsUsed:
Fix for Blackscreen on startup and way higher performance;

Place the file *ATigktxx.dll* from the source's zip into the game's folder – That's literally it.

The DLL is part of AMD's (née ATI's) Catalyst 10.4 software-suite and it *appears*, that this last version was the one, before the ATi's Crossfire-ability broke the game here (CiM).

Source:  

Credit: https://web.archive.org/web/20120111230353/http://www.kn00tcn.net/site/cities-in-motion-startup-crash-fix/  

DLL: https://web.archive.org/web/20120313075602if_/http://www.kn00tcn.net/kn-atigktxx-10.4.zip
notes.verdict: Given the DLL-fix, the game works as usual without issues.
